title: Emergency Fund Calculator: 3-6 Months 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: How Much Emergency Fund Should You Save?
description: Calculate how much to save for emergencies (3-6 months of expenses). Free calculator with 2026 guidelines. No signup required.
intro: Personal finance follows universal principles: [compound interest](/compound-interest-monthly-deposits), diversification, long time horizons, and low costs. Tools that work in any global market. How much should you save for emergencies? Values are updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Emergency Fund Formula: Multiply your average monthly expenses by 3-6 to find your target emergency savings amount.

fields.label: Monthly Expenses
fields.help: Your average monthly expenses (rent, utilities, food, insurance, etc.).

fields.label: Months to Cover

explanation:
Personal finance and investing use universal mathematical principles (compound interest, time value of money, risk-return optimization). This calculator applies standard financial industry formulas.

## How It Works

Your emergency fund should cover 3-6 months of essential expenses. This calculator helps you determine your target amount based on your monthly spending.

This calculator applies the formula with values you enter, checking for typical ranges and alerting you to unusual values.

## Typical Return Benchmarks by Time Horizon

| Time Horizon | S&P 500 | 10Y Bonds | Savings Account |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4-5% | 4-5% |
| 5 years | ~8% average | ~4% | [Inflation rate](/inflation-rate) |
| 10 years | ~9-10% | ~4% | Below inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Key Investment Principles

- **Stay the course during crises.** Emotion is your worst advisor.
- **Diversify:** Don't concentrate in a single asset, sector, or country.
- **Keep costs low:** Fees above 1% annually can eat 30% of your returns over 30 years.
- **Think long-term:** Stocks work over 10+ year horizons, not 1 year.
- **Rebalance annually:** Once per year is sufficient, no more.

## Important Notes

This calculation is a reference tool only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values are updated to 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How much should I save for an emergency fund?
faq.a: Most financial experts recommend 3-6 months of essential expenses. Start with 1 month if that feels overwhelming, then work toward 6 months over time.
Job loss, medical emergencies, major car or home repairs, urgent travel, loss of income. Not vacations, holidays, or planned purchases.

faq.q: Where should I keep my emergency fund?
faq.a: In a liquid, safe account like a high-yield savings account, money market account, or short-term CD. You need quick access without investment risk.

faq.q: Should I build an emergency fund before paying off debt?
faq.a: Save a small cushion ($1,000-2,000) first, tackle high-interest debt next, then build your full 3-6 month emergency fund.

faq.q: How do I calculate my actual monthly expenses?
faq.a: Track all spending for 3 months (rent, utilities, groceries, insurance, etc.), then divide the total by 3. This gives your average.

faq.q: Can I use a credit card for emergencies instead?
faq.a: Not reliably. Credit cards can be declined, frozen, or have limits exactly when you need them most. Keep a separate cash emergency fund.
This provides guideline amounts within ±5%. Use it as a starting point and goal. For personalized advice, consult a financial advisor.

faq.q: Do you collect my personal data?
faq.a: No. All calculations run directly in your browser. We never send any data to our servers.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
